Reading `prd.md` to understand the project requirements and all 12 tasks before any implementation.

[REDACTED]

**Ready.**

I've read the full PRD and understand the project. Here's a concise summary of what we're building:

**EagerMinds Bookmarks** — a personal bookmark manager where users save links, keep some private, and share others publicly via `@handle`.

**Stack:** Next.js 14 (App Router, TypeScript), Supabase (auth + DB + RLS), Resend (welcome email), Tailwind CSS, Vercel.

**Workflow:** Tasks 1–12 in order, one at a time, verify checklist after each, commit after each — no skipping or combining.

| Task | Focus |
|------|--------|
| **1** | Folder structure (empty placeholder files) |
| **2** | Supabase clients (browser/server) + middleware protecting `/dashboard` & `/settings` |
| **3** | DB schema + RLS in Supabase; align `lib/types/index.ts` |
| **4** | Signup + welcome email + `/api/auth/create-profile` (service role) |
| **5** | Login + root page auth redirect; always use `getUser()` not `getSession()` |
| **6** | Bookmarks API CRUD — `user_id` from session only, never from request |
| **7** | Dashboard with full bookmarks CRUD UI |
| **8** | Settings page for handle claim/update (upsert) |
| **9** | Public profile at `/<handle>` (public bookmarks only) |
| **10** | UI polish, error boundaries, `npm run build` must pass |
| **11** | Vercel deploy + Supabase URL config + email confirmation route |
| **12** | README + `/db/schema.sql` |

**Critical security rules noted:**
- Service role key only in server-only routes (e.g. `create-profile`)
- `getUser()` for all auth checks on the server
- RLS backs bookmark API (anon key, not service role)
- Handle format: `^[a-z0-9_]{3,30}$`

Say when to start **Task 1**.

**Task 1 is complete.** The full folder structure is in place with placeholder files — no business logic yet.

### Created structure

**App routes**
- `app/(auth)/login/page.tsx`, `signup/page.tsx`, `confirm/page.tsx`
- `app/(protected)/dashboard/page.tsx`, `settings/page.tsx`
- `app/[handle]/page.tsx` — public profile
- `app/api/auth/confirm/route.ts`
- `app/api/bookmarks/route.ts`, `app/api/bookmarks/[id]/route.ts`
- `app/api/email/welcome/route.ts`
- `app/layout.tsx` (updated), `app/page.tsx` (replaced boilerplate)

**Components**
- `components/auth/` — `LoginForm`, `SignupForm`
- `components/bookmarks/` — `BookmarkCard`, `BookmarkForm`, `BookmarkList`
- `components/ui/` — `Button`, `Input`, `Modal`

**Lib**
- `lib/supabase/` — `client.ts`, `server.ts`, `middleware.ts`
- `lib/resend/` — `client.ts`, `templates/welcome.ts`
- `lib/types/index.ts` — `Bookmark` and `Profile` types

**Root**
- `middleware.ts` — pass-through stub (protection comes in Task 2)

| Check | Status |
|-------|--------|

| `npm run dev` starts without errors | ✅ |

There is a Next.js 16 deprecation notice about `middleware` → `proxy`; it is informational only and will be handled when we implement real middleware in Task 2.

content.text:
**Task 2 is complete.** Supabase is wired for browser, server, and middleware.

### What was implemented

**`lib/supabase/client.ts`**
- `createBrowserClient` from `@supabase/ssr`
- Reads `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`
- Singleton `createClient()` export

**`lib/supabase/server.ts`**
- `createServerClient` with `cookies()` from `next/headers`
- Cookie `getAll` / `setAll` handlers (with try/catch for read-only Server Component context)

**`lib/supabase/middleware.ts`**
- Session refresh via `getUser()` on every request
- Redirects unauthenticated users from `/dashboard` and `/settings` → `/login`
- Public routes (`/`, `/login`, `/signup`, `/confirm`, `/<handle>`) stay open

**`middleware.ts`**
- Delegates to `updateSession`
- Matcher excludes static files and images

**`lib/types/index.ts`** — already matched the PRD; no changes.

### Verification

| Check | Status |
|-------|--------|
| `createBrowserClient` in client.ts | ✅ |
| `createServerClient` + cookies in server.ts | ✅ |
| Middleware protects `/dashboard` & `/settings` | ✅ (307 → `/login`) |
| TypeScript errors | ✅ None |
| `npm run dev` | ✅ Starts cleanly |

**Note:** Removed a stray space after `=` in `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` in `.env.local` — it would have broken the Supabase URL.

Commit when ready:

```bash
git add . && git commit -m "feat: supabase client setup + middleware"
```

Creating the schema file and verifying whether the tables already exist in Supabase.

[REDACTED]

input.contents:
-- =============================================
-- PROFILES TABLE
-- =============================================
create table public.profiles (
  id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade primary key,
  handle text unique not null,
  created_at timestamptz default now() not null
);

-- Enforce handle format: lowercase letters, numbers, underscores, 3-30 chars
alter table public.profiles
  add constraint handle_format check (handle ~ '^[a-z0-9_]{3,30}$');

-- =============================================
-- BOOKMARKS TABLE
-- =============================================
create table public.bookmarks (
  id uuid default gen_random_uuid() primary key,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  title text not null,
  url text not null,
  is_public boolean default false not null,
  created_at timestamptz default now() not null
);

-- =============================================
-- ROW LEVEL SECURITY — PROFILES
-- =============================================
alter table public.profiles enable row level security;

-- Anyone can read any profile (needed for public /<handle> pages)
create policy "Profiles are publicly readable"
  on public.profiles for select
  using (true);

-- Users can only insert their own profile
create policy "Users can create their own profile"
  on public.profiles for insert
  with check (auth.uid() = id);

-- Users can only update their own profile
create policy "Users can update their own profile"
  on public.profiles for update
  using (auth.uid() = id);

-- =============================================
-- ROW LEVEL SECURITY — BOOKMARKS
-- =============================================
alter table public.bookmarks enable row level security;

-- Authenticated users can only read their own bookmarks
create policy "Users can read own bookmarks"
  on public.bookmarks for select
  using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Public bookmarks are readable by anyone (for /<handle> page)
create policy "Public bookmarks are readable by anyone"
  on public.bookmarks for select
  using (is_public = true);

-- Users can only insert bookmarks for themselves
create policy "Users can create own bookmarks"
  on public.bookmarks for insert
  with check (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Users can only update their own bookmarks
create policy "Users can update own bookmarks"
  on public.bookmarks for update
  using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Users can only delete their own bookmarks
create policy "Users can delete own bookmarks"
  on public.bookmarks for delete
  using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- =============================================
-- INDEXES
-- =============================================
create index bookmarks_user_id_idx on public.bookmarks(user_id);
create index bookmarks_public_idx on public.bookmarks(user_id, is_public) where is_public = true;

content.text:
**Task 3 — code side is done.** The schema SQL and TypeScript types are in place; the tables still need to be created in Supabase (one manual step).

### Step 1 — Schema SQL (`db/schema.sql`)

Full PRD schema is saved, including:

- **`profiles`** — `id`, `handle` (unique, `^[a-z0-9_]{3,30}$`), `created_at`
- **`bookmarks`** — `id`, `user_id`, `title`, `url`, `is_public`, `created_at`
- **RLS** on both tables with all 7 policies from the PRD
- **Indexes** — `bookmarks_user_id_idx`, `bookmarks_public_idx`

### Step 2 — Run SQL in Supabase (required)

Tables are **not** in your project yet. Apply the schema:

1. Open [Supabase SQL Editor](https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/bmijtukjpzgulhpwiicm/sql/new)
2. Paste the contents of `db/schema.sql`
3. Click **Run**

Or, if you add your database password to `.env.local`:

```env
SUPABASE_DB_PASSWORD=REDACTED
```

Then run:

```bash
node db/apply-schema.mjs
```

(Password: **Supabase → Project Settings → Database**)

### Step 3 — Types updated (`lib/types/index.ts`)

Types match the table columns:

```typescript
export type Bookmark = {
  id: string;
  user_id: string;
  title: string;
  url: string;
  is_public: boolean;
  created_at: string;
};

export type Profile = {
  id: string;
  handle: string;
  created_at: string;
};
```

### Step 4 — Verify after running SQL

```bash
node db/verify-schema.mjs
```

You should see checks for tables, columns, RLS, and the `handle_format` constraint.

### Checklist

| Item | Status |
|------|--------|
| `db/schema.sql` created | Done |
| `lib/types/index.ts` matches columns | Done |
| Tables in Supabase | **You need to run SQL** |
| RLS policies | **After SQL is applied** |
| TypeScript clean | Done |

After you run the SQL in Supabase, tell me and I’ll re-run verification. Then commit:

```bash
git add . && git commit -m "feat: database schema + RLS policies"
```

input.description: HTTP smoke test signup pages and API routes

content.text:
**Task 4 is complete.** Signup, profile creation, and welcome email are wired end-to-end.

### Signup flow

1. Client calls `supabase.auth.signUp()` with email + password
2. `POST /api/auth/create-profile` with `{ handle }` (session cookie or Bearer token)
3. `POST /api/email/welcome` with `{ email, handle }` (non-fatal on failure)
4. Redirect to `/confirm`

### Files implemented

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `components/auth/SignupForm.tsx` | Form with validation, errors, loading state |
| `app/(auth)/signup/page.tsx` | Centered card layout |
| `app/(auth)/confirm/page.tsx` | “Check your email” message + login link |
| `app/api/auth/create-profile/route.ts` | Session verified via `getUser()`, insert via service role |
| `app/api/email/welcome/route.ts` | Resend welcome email (POST only) |
| `lib/supabase/admin.ts` | Service role client (server-only) |
| `lib/resend/client.ts` | Resend singleton |
| `lib/resend/templates/welcome.ts` | `welcomeEmailHtml(handle)` |

### Security

- **Service role** only in `lib/supabase/admin.ts` → used only by `create-profile`
- **`/api/email/welcome`** uses Resend only — no Supabase keys
- **`create-profile`** requires authenticated user (`getUser()` from cookie or `Authorization: Bearer`)
- **Handle validation** server-side: `/^[a-z0-9_]{3,30}$/`
- **Unique violation** `23505` → `409` “handle already taken”
- **Email errors** logged, return `500` — signup continues (non-fatal)

### Edge case: email confirmation enabled

If Supabase requires email confirmation, there may be no session right after signup → profile creation returns `401`. Handle is stored in user metadata; welcome email still sends and user goes to `/confirm`. They can claim their handle in **Settings** (Task 8).

Also fixed `RESEND_API_KEY` formatting in `.env.local` (spaces around `=` removed).

### Verify manually

1. Visit http://localhost:3000/signup
2. Sign up with a real email, password (8+ chars), and handle (e.g. `het_dev`)
3. Check Supabase → Authentication → Users and `profiles` table
4. Check Resend dashboard → Logs for welcome email
5. Confirm redirect to `/confirm`

Commit when ready:

```bash
git add . && git commit -m "feat: signup flow + welcome email"
```

content.text:
Both issues are fixed.

### 1. Form text not visible
The white card was inheriting light text from dark-mode CSS (`--foreground: #ededed` on a white background).

**Fix:** Explicit dark text on the signup form:
- Headings and labels → `text-gray-900`
- Inputs → `text-gray-900 bg-white`
- Card container → `text-gray-900`

Labels and the “Create your account” heading should now be clearly readable.

### 2. Welcome email — no confirm button
The welcome email was plain text only. It now includes a **“Confirm your email”** button.

**How it works:**
- After signup, the server generates a Supabase confirmation link via `generateLink`
- The welcome email includes a blue **Confirm your email** button plus a fallback plain link
- Clicking it confirms the account and redirects to `/dashboard`
- `/api/auth/confirm` is implemented to handle the redirect

**One Supabase setting required:** Add this redirect URL in your project:

**Supabase → Authentication → URL Configuration → Redirect URLs:**
```
http://localhost:3000/api/auth/confirm
```

Also added `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000` to `.env.local`.

### Test again
1. Hard refresh http://localhost:3000/signup — text should be readable
2. Sign up with a **new email**
3. Check inbox — welcome email should have a blue **Confirm your email** button
4. Click it — should confirm and redirect to dashboard

If the button doesn’t work, confirm the redirect URL is saved in Supabase and restart the dev server so it picks up the new env var.

**Action on your side:** Add `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/confirm` under **Supabase → Authentication → URL Configuration → Redirect URLs**, then sign up with a new email to test the full flow.

Identifying issues: session cookies aren't attached to the redirect response, the welcome email uses Supabase's `action_link` (which can redirect to `/` with errors), and profile creation fails with 401 when there's no session yet. Fixing all three.

input.description: TypeScript check after confirm flow fixes

content.text:
The confirmation flow had three bugs. Here’s what was wrong and what changed:

### Root causes
1. **Session cookies weren’t saved** — `verifyOtp` ran but cookies were written to the wrong place, so the session never persisted → `confirmation_failed`.
2. **Bad confirm link** — The email used Supabase’s `action_link`, which redirected to `/` with `otp_expired` instead of your app’s confirm route.
3. **Profile 401** — With email confirmation on, there’s no session right after signup, so `create-profile` correctly returned 401.

### Fixes applied
- **`/api/auth/confirm`** — Sets auth cookies on the redirect response (required for Supabase SSR).
- **Welcome email** — Button now links directly to  
  `/api/auth/confirm?token_hash=...&type=signup` (no Supabase redirect hop).
- **Profile on confirm** — After email is verified, profile is created from the handle stored in signup metadata.
- **Error handling** — Expired/invalid links on `/` redirect to `/login` with a clear message.

### How to test
1. **Sign up with a new email** (old confirmation links are expired and won’t work).
2. Use the **“Confirm your email” button** in the welcome email (not an older Supabase email if you got two).
3. You should land on `/dashboard` with your profile created.

If it still fails, confirm `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/confirm` is in **Supabase → Authentication → URL Configuration → Redirect URLs**, then restart the dev server.

[REDACTED]

input.description: TypeScript check after signup consolidation

content.text:
**Cause:** Each signup was firing **two** auth emails — Supabase’s `signUp()` confirmation **plus** our Resend welcome (with `generateLink`). After a few test signups, Supabase’s auth email rate limit kicked in.

**Fix:** Signup is now a single server route `POST /api/auth/signup` that:
1. Creates the user via **Admin API** (`createUser`) — **no Supabase auth email**
2. Creates the profile immediately
3. Builds the confirm link via `generateLink` (no email sent)
4. Sends **one** welcome email via Resend with the confirm button

### What to do now
1. **Wait 5–10 minutes** for Supabase’s rate limit to reset (or use a **new email address** you haven’t tried before).
2. Restart dev server if it’s been running a while.
3. Sign up again — you should get **one** email with the confirm button.

If you still see a rate limit error after waiting, it may be **Resend’s** limit — check the [Resend dashboard](https://resend.com/emails) logs. The free tier allows about 100 emails/day.

content.text:
**Fixed.** Signup confirmation now goes through **Supabase Auth email**, which can be delivered to **any email address** — not limited to your Resend account email.

### How it works now

| Environment | Email provider | Recipients |
|---|---|---|
| **Dev (now)** | Supabase Auth | Any email |
| **Production** (optional) | Resend with custom domain | Any email |

- **Default:** Supabase sends the confirmation email via `auth.resend()` — works for all signups.
- **Production:** After you verify a domain on Resend, set in `.env.local`:
  ```env
  RESEND_FROM_EMAIL=Bookmarks <noreply@yourdomain.com>
  ```
  Then the app uses your branded Resend welcome email instead.

### One-time setup (recommended)

Make the Supabase email look like your welcome email:

1. Open **Supabase → Authentication → Email Templates → Confirm signup**
2. Paste the HTML from `supabase/email-templates/confirm-signup.html`
3. Confirm **Redirect URLs** includes:
   ```
   http://localhost:3000/api/auth/confirm
   ```

That template includes a **Confirm your email** button for all users.

### Test it

Sign up with **any email** (not just `hetlimbachiya257@gmail.com`) — they should receive a Supabase confirmation email with a working confirm link.

input.search_term: Supabase custom SMTP setup authentication email 2025
input.explanation: Get current Supabase SMTP configuration steps.

content.text: [REDACTED]
[REDACTED]

Here’s a complete **Supabase + Resend SMTP** setup guide for your Bookmarks app.

---

## Overview

| Method | Who receives emails | When to use |
|--------|---------------------|-------------|
| **Supabase default email** | Anyone (low limit ~2–4/hr) | Local dev, quick testing |
| **Supabase + Resend SMTP** | Anyone (after domain verified) | Production / real users |

Your app already calls `supabase.auth.resend()` on signup. Once SMTP is configured in Supabase, **those emails go through Resend automatically** — no code changes needed.

---

## Step 1 — Verify a domain on Resend

Resend cannot send to arbitrary addresses until you own a domain.

1. Go to [resend.com/domains](https://resend.com/domains)
2. Click **Add Domain** (e.g. `yourdomain.com` or a subdomain like `mail.yourdomain.com`)
3. Add the DNS records Resend shows (usually **SPF**, **DKIM**, sometimes **DMARC**)
4. Wait until status is **Verified** (can take a few minutes to 48 hours)

Use a sender like: `noreply@yourdomain.com` (must match the verified domain).

---

## Step 2 — Get your Resend API key

You already have one in `.env.local` as `RESEND_API_KEY`.

If you need a new one:

1. [resend.com/api-keys](https://resend.com/api-keys) → **Create API Key**
2. Permission: **Sending access**
3. Copy the key (`re_...`) — it’s shown only once

---

## Step 3 — Configure SMTP in Supabase

1. Open your project: [Supabase Dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/bmijtukjpzgulhpwiicm)
2. Go to **Authentication** → **SMTP Settings**  
   (or **Project Settings** → **Authentication** → scroll to **SMTP**)
3. Turn on **Enable Custom SMTP**
4. Fill in:

| Field | Value |
|--------|--------|
| **Sender email** | `noreply@yourdomain.com` *(your verified domain)* |
| **Sender name** | `Bookmarks` |
| **Host** | `smtp.resend.com` |
| **Port** | `465` |
| **Minimum TLS** | `TLS 1.2` |
| **Username** | `resend` *(literally the word `resend`, not your email)* |
| **Password** | Your Resend API key (`re_...`) |

5. Click **Save**

**Resend SMTP reference:**

```
Host:     smtp.resend.com
Port:     465  (SSL) — or 587 / 2587 for STARTTLS
Username: resend
Password: re_your_api_key
```

---

## Step 4 — URL configuration (required for confirm links)

**Authentication** → **URL Configuration**:

| Setting | Local dev | Production |
|---------|-----------|------------|
| **Site URL** | `http://localhost:3000` | `https://your-app.vercel.app` |
| **Redirect URLs** | `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/confirm` | `https://your-app.vercel.app/api/auth/confirm` |

Add both if you use local + Vercel.

---

## Step 5 — Customize the confirmation email template

1. **Authentication** → **Email Templates** → **Confirm signup**
2. Paste the HTML from your repo: `supabase/email-templates/confirm-signup.html`
3. Save

Supabase will use `{{ .ConfirmationURL }}` for the confirm button — that link goes to `/api/auth/confirm` after the user clicks.

---

## Step 6 — Update app env vars (production)

In `.env.local` (and later in Vercel):

```env
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000
RESEND_API_KEY=re_your_api_key

# After domain is verified — enables branded Resend emails from the app API too
RESEND_FROM_EMAIL=Bookmarks <noreply@yourdomain.com>
```

`RESEND_FROM_EMAIL` is optional for signup confirmation once SMTP is set in Supabase (Supabase sends those). It matters for direct Resend API calls (e.g. welcome route).

---

## Step 7 — Adjust rate limits (after SMTP)

**Authentication** → **Rate Limits**

Supabase may cap auth emails at ~30/hour after enabling SMTP. Raise **Email sent** to something reasonable for your app (e.g. 100/hour for dev).

---

## Step 8 — Test

1. Restart dev server: `npm run dev`
2. Sign up with a **new email** (not used before)
3. Check inbox for **Confirm signup** from `Bookmarks <noreply@yourdomain.com>`
4. Click **Confirm your email**
5. You should land on `/dashboard`

Also check:

- [Resend → Emails](https://resend.com/emails) — delivery logs  
- Supabase **Authentication → Users** — user shows as confirmed  

---

## Troubleshooting

| Problem | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| **535 Authentication failed** | Username must be exactly `resend`; password = full API key including `re_` |
| **Emails only to your personal email** | Domain not verified on Resend yet |
| **`otp_expired` / link invalid** | Sign up again for a fresh link; don’t reuse old emails |
| **Redirect to `/` with error** | Add `/api/auth/confirm` to **Redirect URLs** |
| **Email in spam** | Complete SPF/DKIM/DMARC on your domain |
| **No email at all** | Confirm **Enable Custom SMTP** is on; check Resend logs |

---

## Dev without a domain (what you have now)

If you **don’t** have a verified domain yet:

- Leave **Custom SMTP disabled** in Supabase  
- The app uses **Supabase’s built-in email** via `auth.resend()`  
- Works for any address, but with a low hourly limit  

When your domain is verified, enable SMTP (Step 3) and all signups will get confirmation emails through Resend to any address.

---

If you share your domain name (e.g. `eagerminds.com`), I can spell out the exact sender address and env values for your project.

content.text:
Here’s the situation and what to do **without buying a domain**.

---

## Why you’re stuck

Supabase changed the rules (June 2026): on **new free projects**, you **cannot edit auth email HTML** unless you turn on **Custom SMTP**.

So:

- ❌ No domain → can’t use **Resend SMTP** properly (Resend needs a verified domain to send to everyone)
- ❌ No SMTP → can’t paste your HTML in Supabase templates
- ✅ Your app **still sends confirmation emails** via Supabase’s built-in mail (plain default template) — signup can work, just not branded

For the take-home you have **two practical paths**:

---

# Path A — Recommended for now (free, no domain): Gmail SMTP

Use your **personal Gmail** as SMTP. That unlocks custom HTML in Supabase and sends to **any email** (good for dev/demo).

### 1. Create a Google App Password

1. Go to [Google Account → Security](https://myaccount.google.com/security)
2. Turn on **2-Step Verification** (required)
3. Search **App passwords** → Create one
4. Name it `Supabase` → copy the 16-character password (like `abcd efgh ijkl mnop`)

### 2. Configure Supabase SMTP

Open: **Supabase → Authentication → SMTP Settings**

| Field | Value |
|--------|--------|
| **Enable Custom SMTP** | ON |
| **Sender email** | `hetlimbachiya257@gmail.com` *(your Gmail)* |
| **Sender name** | `Bookmarks` |
| **Host** | `smtp.gmail.com` |
| **Port** | `587` |
| **Minimum TLS** | TLS 1.2 |
| **Username** | `hetlimbachiya257@gmail.com` |
| **Password** | The 16-char app password *(no spaces)* |

Click **Save**.

### 3. Set URL configuration

**Authentication → URL Configuration**

| Setting | Value |
|---------|--------|
| **Site URL** | `http://localhost:3000` |
| **Redirect URLs** | `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/confirm` |

### 4. Paste your HTML template

**Authentication → Email Templates → Confirm signup**

Paste everything from `supabase/email-templates/confirm-signup.html` and **Save**.

*(This step only works after SMTP is enabled — that’s why Gmail SMTP comes first.)*

### 5. Raise email rate limit (optional)

**Authentication → Rate Limits** → increase **Emails sent** (e.g. 30–100/hour for testing).

### 6. Test

1. Sign up with a **new email**
2. Inbox should show email from **Bookmarks** via your Gmail
3. Click **Confirm your email** → should land on `/dashboard`

---

# Path B — Skip SMTP & HTML for now (fastest)

If you only need signup to **work** (not pretty emails):

1. Leave **Custom SMTP OFF**
2. Don’t edit the template — use Supabase’s default
3. Your app already calls `supabase.auth.resend()` on signup
4. Users get a plain Supabase confirmation email (works for any address, ~2–4 emails/hour limit)

Good enough to keep building **Task 5 (login)** while you set up Gmail SMTP later.

---

# What NOT to do without a domain

| Approach | Problem |
|----------|---------|
| Resend SMTP + `onboarding@resend.dev` | Only sends to **your** Resend account email |
| Resend API welcome email | Same restriction without domain |
| Buying nothing + expecting branded mail to everyone | Needs SMTP with a real sender (Gmail works for dev) |

---

# Later (production / Vercel deploy)

When you’re ready for real production:

1. Buy a domain (~$10/year) or use one you own  
2. Verify it on [resend.com/domains](https://resend.com/domains)  
3. Switch Supabase SMTP to Resend:

```
Host:     smtp.resend.com
Port:     465
Username: resend
Password: your Resend API key (re_...)
Sender:   noreply@yourdomain.com
```

4. Add to `.env.local` / Vercel:
   ```env
   RESEND_FROM_EMAIL=Bookmarks <noreply@yourdomain.com>
   ```

---

## Quick decision

| Goal | Do this |
|------|---------|
| Branded confirm email + any recipient (dev) | **Path A — Gmail SMTP** (steps above) |
| Just unblock yourself and continue coding | **Path B — default Supabase email** |

---

If you want, tell me which path you picked (**Gmail SMTP** or **default email**) and I can walk you through the next signup test step-by-step or move on to **Task 5 (login page)**.

content.text:
**Done.** Reverted to **Resend-only** emails using your verified domain and fixed the confirm flow.

### What changed

1. **Removed Supabase email fallback** — no more `auth.resend()` from Supabase
2. **All signup/confirm emails** → Resend with your domain:
   ```
   Bookmarks <noreply@bookmarks.driftlessx.dev>
   ```
3. **Fixed `/api/auth/confirm`** — tries multiple OTP types, sets session cookies correctly, marks email confirmed
4. **Added to `.env.local`:**
   ```env
   RESEND_FROM_EMAIL=Bookmarks <noreply@bookmarks.driftlessx.dev>
   ```
5. **Removed** Supabase HTML email template file (not needed with Resend)

### Why you got `confirmation_failed`

You likely clicked a **Supabase SMTP** confirmation link while the app expected a **Resend** link (different token format). Those tokens don’t match → verify fails.

### Important — turn OFF Supabase SMTP

In **Supabase → Authentication → SMTP Settings**:

- Set **Enable Custom SMTP** → **OFF**

Otherwise Supabase may still send its own confirmation emails and conflict with Resend.

Keep these URL settings:

| Setting | Value |
|---------|--------|
| **Site URL** | `http://localhost:3000` |
| **Redirect URLs** | `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/confirm` |

### How to test

1. **Restart dev server** (to load new env vars)
2. Sign up with a **new email** (or reset stuck user):
   ```bash
   node scripts/reset-auth-user.mjs your@email.com
   ```
3. Check inbox — email should be from `noreply@bookmarks.driftlessx.dev`
4. Click **Confirm your email** → should land on `/dashboard`

If no email arrives, use **Resend confirmation** on `/confirm` with your email + password.

input.description: TypeScript check for Task 5

content.text:
**Task 5 is complete.** Login and session handling are in place.

### Implemented

**`components/auth/LoginForm.tsx`** (client)
- Email + password fields
- `signInWithPassword()` via Supabase browser client
- On success → `router.push('/dashboard')` + `router.refresh()`
- On failure → generic **"Invalid email or password"** (no hint which field is wrong)
- Loading state on submit button
- Link to `/signup`

**`app/(auth)/login/page.tsx`**
- Centered card layout (matches signup)
- Shows URL errors (`confirmation_failed`, `link_expired`) from confirm flow

**`app/page.tsx`** (server)
- Auth check via `getUser()` — not `getSession()`
- Logged in → `redirect('/dashboard')`
- Logged out → landing page with **Bookmarks**, tagline, **Sign up** / **Log in** buttons

### Security
- Server auth uses `getUser()` for an authoritative Supabase check
- Login errors are generic to avoid account enumeration

### Verify

| Check | How |
|-------|-----|
| `/login` renders | Visit http://localhost:3000/login |
| Valid credentials → dashboard | Log in with confirmed account |
| Wrong password → error | Use bad password, see inline error |
| `/` when logged in → dashboard | Visit `/` while logged in |
| `/` when logged out → landing | Log out, visit `/` |

Commit when ready:

```bash
git add . && git commit -m "feat: login page + root redirect logic"
```

input.description: Typecheck and verify 401 without auth on all routes

content.text:
**Task 6 is complete.** The bookmarks API is implemented with session-based auth and RLS-backed security.

### Files created/updated

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `lib/api/auth.ts` | `getUser()` helper — never trusts client `user_id` |
| `lib/api/bookmarks.ts` | URL validation (`http://` / `https://` only) |
| `app/api/bookmarks/route.ts` | `GET` list, `POST` create |
| `app/api/bookmarks/[id]/route.ts` | `PUT` update, `DELETE` remove |

### Security rules enforced

- `user_id` always from `getUser()` session — never from body/query
- Unauthenticated requests → **401**
- Updates/deletes scoped with `.eq("id", id).eq("user_id", user.id)` → **404** if not owned
- Uses `createClient` from `lib/supabase/server.ts` (anon key + RLS)
- No service role key in these routes

### API summary

**`GET /api/bookmarks`** — user's bookmarks, `created_at` DESC

**`POST /api/bookmarks`** — `{ title, url, is_public }`
- Title: required, max 200 chars
- URL: valid `http://` or `https://`
- `is_public`: boolean
- Returns **201** with created bookmark

**`PUT /api/bookmarks/:id`** — `{ title?, url?, is_public? }`
- Same validation when fields are present
- Returns **200** or **404**

**`DELETE /api/bookmarks/:id`** — returns **204** or **404**

### Verification

| Check | Status |
|-------|--------|
| GET without auth → 401 | ✅ |
| POST without auth → 401 | ✅ |
| PUT without auth → 401 | ✅ |
| DELETE without auth → 401 | ✅ |
| TypeScript clean | ✅ |

Commit when ready:

```bash
git add . && git commit -m "feat: bookmarks API routes with RLS-backed auth"
```

input.description: TypeScript check for Task 7

content.text:
**Task 7 is complete.** The dashboard with full bookmarks CRUD UI is in place.

### Architecture

**`app/(protected)/dashboard/page.tsx`** (server)
- Auth via `getUser()` → redirect `/login` if missing
- Loads profile handle → redirect `/settings` if missing
- Fetches bookmarks (`user_id`, `created_at` DESC)
- Passes data to `DashboardClient`

**`components/bookmarks/DashboardClient.tsx`** (client)
- Header: **My Bookmarks**, `/@handle` link, **Log out**
- Logout: `signOut()` → `/` + refresh

**`components/bookmarks/BookmarkList.tsx`** (client)
- Local state from `initialBookmarks`
- **New Bookmark** button → opens form modal
- Empty state: *"No bookmarks yet. Add your first one."*
- Optimistic delete with rollback on failure

**`components/bookmarks/BookmarkCard.tsx`**
- Title link (new tab, `rel="noopener noreferrer"`)

content.text:
**Task 8 is complete.** Settings page and handle claiming/updating are in place.

### Implemented

**`app/(protected)/settings/page.tsx`** (server)
- Auth via `getUser()` → redirect `/login` if not authenticated
- Loads current profile from `profiles`
- Renders `SettingsForm` with existing handle (if any)

**`components/auth/SettingsForm.tsx`** (client)
- Shows current `@handle` when set
- Handle input with format validation (`/^[a-z0-9_]{3,30}$/`)
- `POST /api/auth/create-profile` with `{ handle }`
- Success → *"Handle saved! Your profile is live at /{handle}"* (clickable link)
- `409` → *"That handle is taken. Try another."*
- Loading state + **Claim handle** / **Update handle** button

**`app/api/auth/create-profile/route.ts`** (updated)
- **`upsert`** with `onConflict: 'id'` (create or update)
- Still requires authenticated session
- `23505` → `409` handle already taken
- Returns **201** on create, **200** on update

**`app/(protected)/dashboard/page.tsx`**
- Already redirects to `/settings` when no profile row ✅

### Verify

| Check | How |
|-------|-----|
| `/settings` renders | Visit while logged in |
| Claim new handle | User without profile → save handle |
| Taken handle error | Try an existing handle |
| Update handle | Change handle on settings → save |
| Dashboard guard | User without profile → `/dashboard` redirects to `/settings` |

input.description: Typecheck and smoke test public profile routes

content.text:
**Task 9 is complete.** The public profile page at `/<handle>` is live.

### Implemented

**`app/[handle]/page.tsx`** (server, no auth required)
- Looks up profile with case-insensitive `ilike` on handle
- `notFound()` for missing profiles
- Fetches bookmarks: `user_id = profile.id` AND `is_public = true`, newest first
- Shows `@{handle}'s bookmarks` and `{n} public bookmark(s)`
- Each bookmark: title (external link, new tab) + gray URL
- Empty state: *"No public bookmarks yet."*
- Logged-out visitors see **Sign up to create your own**

**`generateMetadata`**
- Title: `@{handle} — Bookmarks`
- Description: `Public bookmarks shared by @{handle}`

**`lib/constants/reserved-handles.ts`**
- Blocks `login`, `signup`, `dashboard`, `settings`, `confirm`, `api` from being treated as handles → `404`

**Middleware**
- Still only protects `/dashboard` and `/settings`
- `/<handle>` stays public (session refresh only)

### Security
- RLS policy *"Public bookmarks are readable by anyone"* — anon key only returns `is_public = true`
- Private bookmarks never appear on this page

### Verify

| Check | How |
|-------|-----|
| `/<handle>` without login | Open in incognito |
| Only public bookmarks | Toggle one public, one private — only public shows |
| Unknown handle → 404 | `/nonexistenthandle123` → 404 ✅ |
| Page `<title>` | Browser tab shows `@{handle} — Bookmarks` |
| `/login` not a profile | Still login page ✅ |

Commit when ready:

```bash
git add . && git commit -m "feat: public profile page /<handle>"
```
